title: Strychnos pernitida
intro: Strychnos peruviana, also known as the Peruvian strychnine tree or Strychnos pernitida, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Loganiaceae. The Peruvian strychnine tree has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and as an antidote for snake bites. The bioactive constituents of Strychnos peruviana include alkaloids such as strychnine and brucine, as well as flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Strychnos pernitida is a member of the family Loganiaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the Loganiaceae family. The Loganiaceae family is part of the order Gentianales. The order Gentianales is a group of flowering plants that also includes families such as Apocynaceae and Rubiaceae. The classification of Strychnos pernitida is further supported by its characteristics and morphological features.
